summary_one_line: A processing instruction (<?...?>) must appear inside an internal DTD subset so ctxt->inSubset==1 holds at the PI handler.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must contain a processing instruction emitted while the parser is inside the internal DTD subset, i.e. a `<?...?>` between `<!DOCTYPE name [` and the closing `]>`, so that ctxt->inSubset == 1 when xmlSAX2ProcessingInstruction runs and the true branch at L2740 is taken (then xmlAddChild to intSubset at L2741).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The naive_ctx winner resolves 8/10 and takes the true side (L2740 T=2 in W vs T=0 in L); its seed carries a well-formed `<?xml version="1.0"?>` prolog plus a `<!DOCTYPE a...` declaration, the structural scaffold needed to enter inSubset==1 when a PI is parsed inside the subset. Context-sensitive coverage rewards the PI handler reached via the DTD-internal-subset call context as a distinct new edge, so the path into the subset PI handler stays in the corpus and gets driven to the inSubset==1 site.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 10/10 and only ever takes the false side (L2740 T=0 F=91 in L); it reaches xmlSAX2ProcessingInstruction 91 times but with inSubset==0, so it falls through to the parent/root handling at L2747-L2755 (the [L] lines). Its inputs have the DOCTYPE/PI fragments mangled and never assemble a PI nested inside an open internal subset, so the inSubset==1 state never co-occurs with a PI parse.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The only technique delta between naive_ctx and naive is the CtxHook: naive_ctx folds a running call-context hash into the edge-map index, so reaching xmlSAX2ProcessingInstruction via the internal-subset parse path is a fresh (call-context, edge) coverage bucket that naive's bare-edge map cannot distinguish from the much more frequent root-level PI path. This context-sensitive coverage keeps the subset-PI input alive in the corpus and steers mutation toward the inSubset==1 true branch; the mutators are identical havoc/token (no I2S, no CMP_MAP).

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of `if (ctxt->inSubset == 1)` in xmlSAX2ProcessingInstruction, whose true arm adds the PI to the internal subset.
The winner takes the true side of the blocker while the loser only takes the false side and proceeds to the parent/root handling.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2740  T=2 F=0  T=0 F=91  if (ctxt->inSubset == 1) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The winning seed contains a well-formed XML prolog and a DOCTYPE declaration, the structural scaffold for entering an internal subset.
The loser inputs reach the PI handler 91 times but always with the non-subset state, never the inSubset==1 true side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2740  T=2 F=0  T=0 F=91  if (ctxt->inSubset == 1) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx differs from naive only by a CtxHook that folds the call-context into the edge-map index, making (call-context, edge) pairs the coverage unit.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
keeps a running hash of the current call context (caller chain) and
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx uses the same havoc/token mutator stack as naive with no I2S or CMP_MAP, so the divergence is attributable to the coverage granularity, not comparison solving.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive's havoc + token stack. No `I2SRandReplace`, no
CMP_MAP.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Lineage verification is not mandatory for context-sensitive coverage, but I ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 7476 --role W --fuzzer naive_ctx --trial 1 --seed fbd4d48bc7f5a45b` as a sanity check. The 8-level chain is all havoc/token ops (BytesExpandMutator, TokenInsert, CrossoverReplaceMutator, etc.); the single dash row at depth 7 is the imported-root ancestor, not an I2S find, consistent with MECHANISM CONTEXT stating naive_ctx has no I2SRandReplace/CMP_MAP and no ParentInfo-only dash rows. The winner mechanism is the CtxHook coverage granularity, not comparison solving.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the PI handler is reached from only one call context (no root-level vs subset-level path ambiguity) would show naive_ctx and naive resolving the inSubset==1 site at the same rate, killing the context-sensitivity attribution.

weakest_evidence_point: The claim that naive's bare-edge map conflates the subset-PI path with the root-level PI path is inferred from the 91 root-level PI hits and the CtxHook description, not directly observed as a collided map slot.
